type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
2012 NOx, CO and VOC emission volumes have
increased by greater than 10 percent when compared to 2011. These increases are attributed to an expansion of the facility which involved the addition of Phase III. Phase
III of the expansion involved doubling the amount of stationary combustion equipment which consequently resulted in an increase of CAC emission volumes.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The 03-22-078-12 gas plant had a brand new phase come online in August of 2017, so production levels are higher for the 2017 reporting period. The bump in production is in line with the bump in NO2 emissions

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: This is the first year that the Pouce Coupe 03-22-078-12 plant has 10+ full time employees, therefore 2017 reporting year is the first time that these fugitive emissions need to be reported.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The 03-22-078-12 gas plant had a brand new phase come online in August of 2017, so production levels are higher for the 2017 reporting period. The rise in production is in line with the rise in CO emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The two major sources for the change in quantities released from last year are:
1) A change in production levels. A new phase of the gas plant was brought online in August of 2017, so lots more production went through the plant.
2) In previous years, we have used a third party to submit the NPRI reports on our behalf. They may have used different estimation methods.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: 2017 reporting year was the first year that the 03-22-078-12 gas plant met the thresholds to report non-combustion sources of emissions. Most of the VOC's come from the fugitive emissions (non-combustion), so this is the first year that the gas plant has met the threshold criteria.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The majority of SO2 emissions result from flaring acid gas and sour gas. Production volumes through the facility remained relatively steady, but increased flaring of sour and acid gas resulted in a ~38% increase in SO2 emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: As part of VOC Speciation, Formehldehyde was generated on site over the course of the year through various activities such as combustion from engines/heaters, flaring, fugitives, etc. No single release point or purposefully generated formeldehyde is on site.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: N/A as there were no disposals

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: N/A as there was no recycling (not necessary)

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The vast majority of SO2 released at the facility comes from flaring of sour or acid gas. There was less sour/acid gas flared in 2020 than in 2019 of 19.8% due to less process shutdowns taking place in 2020. In 2019, new phases of the facility were constructed and commissioned which takes some time to get running smoothly, therefore requires more flaring of sour gas. Still not a very big change, but it is more than the 10% noted above.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: TPM emissions did not meet the 20 tonne threshold, but were still reported as every other Part 4 substance surpassed the NPRI reporting threshold.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: There was a slight increase in production from the previous year, but what pushed this over th 10% threshold was the composition of gasses being combusted by the flare stacks. More sour gas (higher H2S concentrations) were flared resulting in a 21% increase in SO2 emissions.
